summary: DHH responds to a Mobitopia post criticizing telecom companies for their MMS pricing strategy. The original post argued that telcos were 'half ass guessing' rather than using cost-plus pricing models. DHH dismisses this criticism, suggesting the commenter doesn't understand basic economics and pricing theory. The post is a brief teaser linking to a longer argument on his blog.
key_points: Telcos were being criticized for not using cost-plus pricing models for MMS
DHH argues that cost-plus pricing isn't the only 'real' profit model, implying value-based or market-based pricing is equally valid
The post is a brief fragment teasing a longer economics lesson on pricing theory
money_quotes: It sounds like Russell Beattie needs a brief introduction to economics and pricing. It's actually really simple, so I won't even charge tuition.
key_insight: Cost-plus pricing isn't the only legitimate pricing model — DHH suggests telcos' MMS pricing reflects market-based economics rather than ignorance.
